scenarios.title: Suspicious Payment Request
scenarios.narrative: It's 4:45pm on a Friday. Your finance officer receives an urgent email — apparently from a senior director — asking for an immediate wire transfer to a 'new supplier' before the bank closes. The tone is urgent and the sender insists this can't wait until Monday.

options.label: Approve immediately to avoid delays
options.feedback: Acting under pressure without verification is exactly how business email compromise scams succeed.

options.label: Verify the supplier and request through a known channel
options.feedback: Excellent. Verifying out-of-band is the single best defence against payment fraud.

options.label: Escalate internally before acting
options.feedback: Good instinct — escalation slows down fraud, though direct verification is even stronger.

options.label: Ignore the email entirely
options.feedback: Ignoring it avoids the immediate risk, but doesn't address the underlying process gap.

scenarios.title: Lost Laptop
scenarios.narrative: A sales employee leaves a company laptop in a taxi after a client meeting. It contained customer contracts, pricing data, and saved email credentials.

options.label: Device was encrypted
options.feedback: Encryption ensures data on the device can't be read even if it's lost — a strong baseline control.

options.label: Remote wipe was enabled
options.feedback: The best outcome — you can remotely erase company data the moment it's reported missing.

options.label: No controls were in place
options.feedback: Without encryption or remote wipe, all data on that laptop is now exposed.

options.label: It was a personal device with no MDM
options.feedback: Unmanaged personal devices accessing company data is a significant, common SME risk (BYOD risk).

scenarios.title: Employee Uses AI Tool
scenarios.narrative: An employee wants to speed up a report and pastes a spreadsheet of confidential customer data into a public AI chatbot to summarise it.

options.label: No restrictions on AI tool usage
options.feedback: Without any AI policy, confidential data can easily leak into third-party AI training sets.

options.label: A clear AI usage policy exists and is enforced
options.feedback: Excellent — a documented, enforced AI policy is the foundation of responsible AI governance.

options.label: AI usage is restricted to approved, vetted tools
options.feedback: Restricting to vetted tools with data protections greatly reduces leakage risk.

options.label: Unsure if a policy exists
options.feedback: Uncertainty about AI policy usually means there isn't one — this is a governance gap to close.

scenarios.title: Website Outage
scenarios.narrative: Your e-commerce website goes down during your biggest sales weekend of the year. Customers can't check out and social media complaints are starting.

options.label: Restore quickly from a recent backup
options.feedback: Having tested, recent backups means downtime is minutes, not days.

options.label: Contact the hosting provider for support
options.feedback: A reasonable step, but slower without your own backup and recovery plan.

options.label: Pay the attacker demanding ransom
options.feedback: Paying ransom funds criminals and doesn't guarantee recovery — never the right first move.

options.label: No backup exists
options.feedback: Without backups, an outage can mean permanent data loss and extended downtime.

scenarios.title: CEO WhatsApp Scam
scenarios.narrative: Your finance manager receives a WhatsApp message from a number claiming to be the CEO, urgently requesting a confidential fund transfer and asking to keep it discreet.

options.label: Verify by calling the CEO directly
options.feedback: Direct voice verification through a known number defeats nearly all impersonation scams.

options.label: Transfer the funds as requested
options.feedback: This is the classic 'CEO fraud' scam pattern — never transfer funds based on an unverified message.

options.label: Ask finance colleagues for a second opinion
options.feedback: Good collaborative instinct, though direct verification with the CEO is stronger.

options.label: Escalate to management before acting
options.feedback: Escalating slows the scam down and is a solid process control.
